Read only canonical first-party pages that load in the browser. Return the exact page URLs visited and concise extracted findings tied to those pages. Prefer docs.devin.ai and official Devin/Cognition pages. Do not use search-result snippets.

raw_result.description: Devin is an autonomous AI software engineer with official docs for interactive use, workflow automation, and a public API.
raw_result.auth_notes: The official API authentication docs describe API keys for service users and personal access tokens in closed beta; both are bearer-style tokens prefixed with "cog_".
raw_result.gating_reason: The docs show paid plans such as Pro, Max, Teams, and Enterprise for product access and billing, indicating toolkit use requires a paid plan even though a Free plan exists for the product.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs include a public API reference with overview, authentication, pagination, common flows, and organization/enterprise scopes for managing Devin workflows and related resources.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official docs include a Devin MCP page and reference the official MCP server at mcp.devin.ai.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit appears buildable from the documented REST API, but access/auth is centered on service-user API keys and personal access tokens are still in closed beta, so setup may require creating service-user credentials inside a paid Devin workspace.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: The docs evidence indicates rate limiting exists because the API references HTTP 429 Too Many Requests, but the gathered pages did not expose specific numeric limits.
raw_result.notable_signals: The documentation is well structured and includes API concepts, auth, common flows, billing/usage material, and official MCP support, which are strong signals of agent-toolkit viability.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The exact access tier and buildability hinge on plan availability for API credentials: the gathered evidence shows paid plans and service-user token setup, but the browser evidence did not capture explicit wording on whether the Free plan includes API access, so access_tier and verdict are supported only at a workaround level rather than fully self-serve ready today.
